name: Utah County

actions.title: (Eagle Mountain) Data center campus approval and development plans
actions.summary: Eagle Mountain City approved plans for large-scale data center development in the city, part of a broader push to attract hyperscale data center campuses to Utah County's west side.

contextBlurb: Utah County's western cities, particularly Eagle Mountain, have seen significant interest from data center developers attracted by available land and growing infrastructure. Water scarcity and power grid capacity remain key concerns for residents and planners.

name: Salt Lake County

actions.title: (West Jordan) Data center zoning and development review
actions.summary: West Jordan and other Salt Lake County municipalities have reviewed zoning updates to accommodate growing demand for data center construction, balancing economic development with residential concerns.

contextBlurb: Salt Lake County has long been a hub for data center activity in the Wasatch Front corridor. Increasing demand from AI workloads has intensified discussions about power consumption, water use for cooling, and appropriate zoning near residential areas.

name: Tooele County

actions.title: Data center development proposals on former industrial land
actions.summary: Tooele County has fielded proposals for large data center campuses on available industrial and brownfield land west of Salt Lake City, with discussions around water rights and power supply.

contextBlurb: Tooele County's vast open land and proximity to Salt Lake City's infrastructure make it attractive for data center siting, but water scarcity in the Great Salt Lake basin and power transmission limitations are significant concerns.
